Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Goal Create a Python dictionary representing a Log Skeleton for a specific order fulfillment process scenario Input A list of activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP and a description of the process flow Output Format A Python dictionary with specific keys equivalence always_before always_after never_together activ_freq directly_follows and specific value types sets of tuples or dict of sets Context A complex order fulfillment process in a distribution center Analyze the Scenario Activities RO Receive Order Start CA Check Availability RI Reserve Items PI Pick Items QC Quality Check PO Pack Order GSL Generate Shipping Label D Dispatch IC Invoice Customer RP Record Payment Logical Flow Inference Order comes in RO Check if stock exists CA If yes reserve stock RI Pick items from shelves PI Check quality QC Pack items PO Generate label GSL Dispatch D Invoice IC after packing labeling before payment Record Payment RP after invoice

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Inferring the logical flow from the scenario description RO is the start CA RI PI QC PO GSL D is the core physical flow IC usually happens after PO GSL RP after IC Assume linear flow with some parallelism or strict ordering for standard skeleton

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Drafting the Constraints directly_follows Strict sequence RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D PO IC IC RP always_before RO D CA D RI RP equivalence RO CA GSL D never_together empty since all activities coexist in successful case activ_freq all 1

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Refining the Flow revising chain for IC RP considering PO IC or GSL IC self correction on never_together deciding empty set after considering alternatives revising activ_freq considering QC 1 2 but sticking to 1 refining directly_follows to include fork PO GSL and PO IC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining the Constraints for Validity checking directly follows logic PO splits to GSL D and IC RP always before RO D CA RP always after D RO RP CA equivalence RO CA GSL D activ_freq all 1 Final Review of the Logic checking if RO before CA CA before RI etc all yes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interpreting constraint definitions Always Before If first occurs second previously so B A Always After If first occurs second following so A B aligning dictionary with prompt definitions for RO D etc

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Using logical process knowledge to place IC after PO RP after IC assuming fork structure for realism while keeping consistent with scenario
